{"step_by_step_thinking": "Neurofilament light chain (NfL) is a biomarker that reflects axonal damage and neurodegeneration. Multiple documents confirm its role in various neurological conditions. For example, in spinal cord injuries, elevated NfL levels correlate with injury severity and neurological outcomes. In traumatic brain injury, NfL is one of the six biomarkers compared in studies to predict the need for CT imaging. In frontotemporal dementia, CBD, and motor neuron disease, NfL is elevated and reflects neurodegeneration. Document [14] specifically states that NfL is a marker historically used to assess disease progression, and tofersen's mechanism involves reducing NfL levels. Document [4] notes that NfL is a marker of neuroaxonal loss. Document [17] and [22] also mention NfL as a marker of neurodegeneration in the context of dementia. These consistent references across multiple conditions and contexts confirm that NfL is a validated biomarker of neurodegeneration.", "answer_choice": "A"}
